title: siparuna neglecta
intro: Siparuna neglecta, also known as the "Guayabilla" or "Siparuna," is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the family Siparunaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Siparuna neglecta include relief from fever, pain, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections. Siparuna neglecta can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
